Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Compliance With Deposition Subpoena And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jan-15-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Plaintiff Marion Fisher's motion to compel compliance with deposition subpoena is ordered off calendar for failure to comply with California Rule of Court 3.1025. A written notice and all moving papers supporting a motion to compel production of documents from a non-party deponent must be personally served on the non-party deponent unless they have agreed to accept service by mail at an address specified on the deposition record. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN. Not reported.

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Production Of Documents & For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jan-11-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCE:
J. Scott Weaver, Esq., for plaintiff and moving party: Marion Nathaniel Fisher
David E. Young, Esq., for defendant and opposing party: Sukhdevji D. Patel

AFTER HEARING, TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Plaintiff's motion to compel further responses to request for production of documents is Granted. The objections, other than based on the attorney client or attorney work product privilege are overruled and stricken. Defendants are ordered to serve verified supplemental responses and produce documents within 15 days of notice of this order. If Defendants are unable to comply with a particular demand, they shall fully comply with CCP Section 2031.230. If any documents are withheld pursuant to a claim of privilege, they shall be identified with particularity in a privilege log. The Court finds that defendants asserted unmeritorious objections and made evasive responses. Monetary sanctions in the amount of $1,165 are to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. Order signed in open court. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN. Not reported.

Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Specially Prepared Interrogatories And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jan-11-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCE:
J. Scott Weaver, Esq., for plaintiff and moving party: Marion Nathaniel Fisher
David E. Young, Esq., for defendant and opposing party: Sukhdevji D. Patel

AFTER HEARING, TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Plaintiff Marion Fisher's motion to compel further responses to special interrogatories is Granted.
The objections are overruled and stricken. Defendants are ordered to serve verified supplemental responses within 15 days of notice of this order. If defendants respond pursuant to CCP Section 2030.230, the responsive documents shall be identified with particularity. The Court finds that defendants asserted unmeritorious objections and made evasive responses. Monetary sanctions in the amount of $1,165 are to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. Order signed in open court. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN. Not reported.
